text: BALLINOCKEN, a village in the p. of Fermagh, co. Carlow. Pop. 169.
BALLINODE, a village in the p. of Tydavnet, co. Monaghan, 4 m. NW of Monaghan. Pop. 245.
BALLINOE, a parish, containing a village of the same name, in co. Cork, 5 m. W by S of Tallow. Area 7,716 acres. Pop. 2,566. Pop. of the v. 365.

BALLINROBE, a parish, partly in co. Galway, but chiefly in co. Mayo. The Mayo section, which contains the town of B., has an area of 17,510 acres. Pop. 10,115. The Galway section, separated by Lough Mask from the Mayo section, bears the name of Churchfield, and has an area of 9,362 acres. Pop. 1,035. A summit in the extreme W, and one near the centre of the Galway section, have altitudes of respectively 1,697 and 1,201 ft.—The post and market town of B. stands on the river Robe, 45 m. SW by S of Hollymount. Though inferior to Ballinasloe, Westport, and Ballina, it markedly excels the majority of Connaught towns in regularity, cleanliness, and amount of apparent comfort; and numerous remains of churches and castles in the circumjacent country, both in and beyond the parish, seem to indicate that the town anciently possessed considerable importance. Pop. in 1831, 2,604; in 1841, 2,678. Houses 488.
BALLINRODE. See DUNGARVAN.
BALLINSKELLIGS. See BALLINASKELLIGS.
BALLINSPITTLE, a village in the p. of Ringrove, 31 m. SW of Kinsale, in co. Cork. Pop. 126.
BALLINTAMPLE, a village in the p. of Ahamplish, 8 m. N by W of Sligo, co. Sligo. Pop. 178.

BALLINTEMPLE, a parish 4½ m. NW of Arklow, in co. Wicklow. Area 4,087 acres. Pop. 1,114. —Also a parish 4 m. NE of Tipperary, co. Tipperary. Area 4,208 acres. Pop. 656.—Also a parish 2 m. ENE of Middleton, co. Cork. Within this p. is the v. of Churchtown. Pop. 111.—Also a village in the p. of St. Finbar's, in co. Cork, on the S side of the river Lee, 11 m. E of Cork. Pop. 455.—Also a parish 6 m. SSW of Cavan. Area 10,658 acres. Pop. 5,341.

BALLINTOBBER, a parish, containing a village of the same name, in co. Roscommon. Area 6,352 acres. Pop. 2,616.—The v. stands on the road between Castlerea and Roscommon, about 4 m. SE of the former. A little W of it, on the extremity of a narrow and declivitous ridge, stand the ruins of the castle of B., a quadrangular enclosure of 270 by 237 ft. within walls, defended by 4 strong towers at the angles, and 2 at the sides of the grand entrance, and surrounded by a broad fosse. This castle was the residence of the O'Conors, kings of Connaught, and figured, down to a comparatively recent date, in some stirring scenes both of their own history and of that of their descendants.—Also a parish 7 m. NNW of Ballinrobe, co. Mayo. Area 22,966 acres. Pop. 7,199. Slieve-Bohaan, on the S boundary, has an alt. of 1,294 ft.; and the river Ayle, while connected with this p., descends from an elevation of 282 ft. to the level of Lough Carra.
BALLINTOGHER, a village in the p. of Killery, co. Sligo. Pop. 234.

BALLINTOY, a parish, containing a village of the same name, on the N coast of co. Antrim. It extends 5 m. along the shore. Area 12,753 acres. Pop. 4,816. Croaghmore, or 'the Large stack,' rises abruptly in this p. to the height of 471 ft. above sealevel. The v. of B. is 4 m. NW of Ballycastle. Pop. 310. It occupies a bold and romantic site on an impressively barren and denuded shore.

BALLINTRA, a village in the p. of Drumholm, 2 M PARALANT PROPERTURA DE PROPERTURA DE PROPERTURA DE PROPERTURA DE PROPERTURA DE PROPERTURA DE PROPERTURA DE PROPERTURA DE PROPERTURA DE PROPERTURA DE PROPERTURA DE PROPERTURA DE PORTA DE PROPERTURA DE PORTA DE PORTA DE PORT co. Donegal, 41 m. NNE of Ballyshannon. Pop. 522.

BALLINURE, a parish in co. Wicklow, 5<sup>1</sup>/<sub>4</sub> m. N by W of Baltinglass. Area 7,404 acres. Pop. 1,718. The highest ground in this p. has an alt. of 1,023 ft. -Also a parish in co. Antrim. It contains the village of B., and part of the post-town of Ballyclare. Area 8,540 acres. Pop. 3,469. The village of B. is 2½ m. NE of Ballyclare. Pop. 380.
BALLINVOHER, a parish on the N shore of Dingle bay, co. Kerry. Area 16,661 acres. Pop 3,579.
BALLISAKEERY. See Balisakeery.
BALLISODERE. See BALLYSADERE.
BALLITORE. See BALLYTORE.
BALLIVOR. See KILBALIVER.

BALLOCH, a hamlet and ferry in the p. of Bonhill, Dumbartonshire, a little below the issue of the Leven from Loch Lomond.—Also a small sheet of water at the foot of Torlum, in the p. of Muthil, in Perthshire.

BALLOCHMYLE, a hamlet and locality of poetical celebrity, on the N bank of the Ayr, in Ayrshire, 2 m. from Mossgiel, the early home of the poet Burns. The Cumnock extension of the Ayrshire railway here crosses the river by a noble viaduct, the central arch of which has a span of 180 ft., and rises 150 ft. above the bed of the stream.

BALLON, a canton, commune, and village of France, in the dep. of Sarthe, arrond. of Le Mans.— The cant. comprises 12 com.—The com. on the Orne river has a pop. of 4,078.—Also a com. in the dep. of Charente-Inferieure, cant. of Aigrefeuille. Pop. 669.
BALLON, a parish, containing a v. of the same name, 31 m. S by W of Tullow, co. Carlow. Area 3,700 acres. Pop. 1,524. Pop. of v. 327.
BALLOTS, a commune of France, in the dep. of Mayenne, cant. of Saint-Aignan-sur-Roe. Pop. 1,320.
BALLRECHTAN, a village of Baden, in the circle of the Upper Rhine, 3 m. SW of Staufen. Pop. 433. Iron and lead are mined in the vicinity.
BALLRICHAN. See BALRAHAN.
BALL'S BRIDGE, a village in the p. of St. Mary, Donnybrook, co. Dublin, on the rivulet Dodder, 13 m. from Dublin-castle. There are extensive calicoprinting works here. Pop. 484.
BALLSTADT, a town of Coburg-Gotha, in the bailiwick of Tonna. Pop. 632.
BALLSTON, a township in Saratoga co., in the state of New York, U.S., 26 m. from Albany, on the Schenectadey and Saratoga railroad. Pop. 2,050.

BALLSTON-SPA, the cap. of Saratoga co., in the state of New York, U. S., in the township of Milton, 28 m. from Albany, on a branch of the Kayaderosseras creek. Pop. 1,500. It is celebrated for its mineral springs.
BALLYADAMS, a parish in Queen's co. Area 6,908 acres. Pop. 2,051.
BALLYAGRAN, a village in the p. of Corcomohide, co. Limerick. Pop. 133.
BALLYANE, or BALLYANNE, a parish on the W border of co. Wexford, 2½ m. NNE of New Ross. Area 4,578 acres. Pop. 1,292.

BALLYBACON, a parish 4 m. E by S of Clogheen, co. Tipperary. Area 11,120 acres. Pop. 3,109. The chief summits have alts. of 1,718 ft., 1,846 ft., 2,101 ft., and 2,609 ft.; and the highest of these is Knockmeledown-proper, on the southern boundary.
BALLYBARRACK, a parish 1 m. SSW of Dundalk. Area 1,018 acres. Pop. 301.

BALLYBAY, or BALLIBAY, a parish and town in co. Monaghan. Area of p. 8,741 acres. Pop. 6,606. -The town of B. stands in the Cremourne section of the parish, 5 m. W by N of Castleblaney. Pop. 1,768. BAL
BALLYBODIN, a village in the p. of Whitechurch, co. Dublin. Pop. 252.
BALLYBOFEY, a small market-town, in the p. of Stranorlar, co. Donegal, on the river Finn, 111 m. W by S of Strabane. Pop. 782.
BALLYBOGGAN, a parish 2 m. SW of Clonard, co. Meath. Area 6,222 acres. Pop. 1,430.
BALLYBOGHILL, a parish 33 m. NNW of Swords, co. Dublin. Area 2,789 acres. Pop. 595.
BALLYBORRIS. See Borris.
BALLYBOUGH, a village in the p. of Clonturk, co. Dublin, on the N side of the small estuary of the Ballybough, 14 m. NE of Dublin. Area 112 acres. Pop. 1,074.
BALLYBOUGHT, a parish in co. Kildare, 63 m. SW by S of Blessington. Area 1,441 acres. Pop. 265.
BALLYBOY, a parish in King's co., containing the villages of Ballyboy and Frankford. Area 14,274 acres. Pop. 4,753. B. village is 94 m. NE of Birr. Pop. 348.
BALLYBOYS, a parish 3 m. ENE of Dundalk, in co. Louth. Area 1,436 acres. Pop. 748.
BALLYBRACK, a village in the p. of Rossmere, co. Waterford. Pop. in 1831, 165.
BALLYBRACKEN. See KILBRACKEN.
BALLYBRAZIL, or Ballybrassil, a parish 44 m. SE of New Ross, co. Wexford. Area 2,371 acres. Pop. 567.
BALLYBRENNAN, a parish 5 m. SSE of Wexford, co. Wexford. Area 1,041 acres. Pop. 260.
BALLYBRICKEN, or Cahirelly, a parish of a mile NE of Six-mile-Bridge, co. Limerick. Area 2,719 acres. Pop. 1,329.
BALLYBRITTAS, a small post-v. in the parish of Lea, Queen's co., 2 m. NE of Emo. Pop. 136.
BALLYBROMMELL, a village in the p. of Fennagh, co. Carlow. Pop. 166.
BALLYBROOD, a parish 4 m. NE of Six-mile- Bridge, co. Limerick. Area 2,355 acres. Pop. 1,121.

BALLYBUNIAN, a village and celebrated caves, in the sea-board p. of Killeheney, co. Kerry. The v. stands on the estuary of the Shannon, 2 m. N by E of the mouth of the Cashen river, 103 m. NE by E of Kerry-head. Pop. 271. It has of late years become a considerable sea-bathing resort, and a favourite post of observation to tourists. The caves of B. are situated on the Kerry side of the mouth of the Shannon, between the small estuary of the Cashen and Kilconly-point. They are less remarkable for their dimensions than for the singular form of the rocks, which seem as if carved by the hand of man. They are in some instances semicircularly arranged, like the groin-work of an arch, or are piled above one another in regular succession.
BALLYBUR, a small parish 3 m. from Kilkenny, in co. Kilkenny. Area 667 acres. Pop. 200.
BALLYBURLEY, or PRIMULT AND COOLCOR, an united parish in King's co. Area 7,868 acres. Pop. 1,621.
BALLYCAHANE, a parish 3 m. N by E of Croom, co. Limerick. Area 3,738 acres. Pop. 1,196.
BALLYCAHILL, a parish 3 m. W of Thurles, co. Tipperary. Area 3,844 acres. Pop. 1,903.
BALLYCALLEN, a parish 44 m. WSW of Kilkenny, in co. Kilkenny. Area 6,835 acres. Pop. 1,778.
BALLYCANEW, a parish, containing a village of the same name, in co. Wexford. Area 3,628 acres. Pop. 1,275.
BALLYCARANEY, or BALLYCRANE, a parish 8 m. SSE of Rathcormuck, in co. Cork. Area 3,939 acres. Pop. 1,160.
BALLYCARNEY, a village and a parish on the banks of the Slaney, in co. Wexford. The village is 5 m. W by S of Ferns. Area of p. 8,234 acres. Pop. 2,045.
BALLYCARRY, a village in the p. of Templecorran, co. Antrim, 41 m. NNE of Carrickfergus. Pop. 236.

BALLYCASSIDY, a rivulet of the counties of Tyrone and Fermanagh. It rises near Dromore, runs 71 m. SSW, and then 5 m. to Lough Erne, which it enters at a point 5 m. N of Enniskillen, near a hamlet of the same name. A canal projected some years ago from Lough Foyle to Lough Erne, has its western terminus at the village.

BALLYCASTLE, a chapelry lying around Ballycastle bay, and containing the town of Ballycastle, within the p. of Ramoan, co. Antrim. The post, market, and seaport town of B. is picturesquely situated on the shore, at the foot of the monarch-mountain of Knock-lead, 31 m. E of Fair Head. It consists of an upper and a lower town lying upwards of 4 of a mile asunder, and connected by a straight umbrageous handsome avenue. The lower town is called the Quay, and contains a pile which was once used as a custom-house, but is now occupied as a barrack. The upper town is the larger and more important section. The scenery, from numerous spots in the environs, possesses a splendour and variety of tint, a richness of composition, and a combined force and beauty of character superior to that of almost any other town in Ireland. This place did not acquire importance or become a town or seat of trade till about 1770; and it then sprang suddenly into bulk and strength, and an attempt was made to make it a port and place of export for coals. But the bay is particularly exposed to the NW winds, which, encountering the island of Raghery in their way, drive in a tremendous sea between that island and B.; and while the shipping were without protection, it was in vain to expect B. could become a place of commerce. The violence of the tides overthrew the piers; the harbour was choked up with drifted sand; and a trade, which had arisen with suddenness, extent, and energy, was instantly flung prostrate, and, to all appearance, irretrievably destroyed. The exports of B. were coal; earth used in china manufactories, equivalent to kaolin, and procured on Fair Head; granite from Ballypatrick mountain, resembling petunsee, also used in the composition of china; potters' clay, brick clay, and excellent fire-stones; sand for glassmaking; kelp, and manganese. To these may be added freestone, limestone, and flints. The trade of the town was not quite extinguished by the ruin of the harbour; and, in 1835, amounted, in the estimated value of exports and imports, to respectively £1,791, and £2,030 13s. 3d. The exports consisted of kelp, corn and meal, potatoes, cattle, and untanned calf-skins; and the imports of coals, culm, cinders, iron, slates, oak-bark, salt, tinned plates, and other articles. Pop. in 1831, 1,683; in 1841, 1,697.
BALLYCASTLE, a small bay and a fishing-village in the p. of Dunfeeny, co. Mayo. The bay opens about 11 m. SW of Downpatrick-head. The v. stands on the shore of the bay, 8 m. NW of Killalla. Pop. 798.
BALLYCLARE, a market and post town, partly in the p. of Ballinure, but chiefly in that of Doagh Grange, co. Antrim. It stands on Six-mile-Water, 21 m. SW of Ballinure. Pop. 847.
BALLYCLERAHAN, a parish, containing a village of the same name, 4 m. SW of Fethard, co. Tipperary. Area 1,044 acres. Pop. 728.
BALLYCLOG, a parish 2 m. N of Stewartstown, co. Tyrone. Area 7,796 acres. Pop. 2,697. The surface is low, and declines to the W shore of Lough Neagh.

BALLYCLOUGH, a parish, containing a small cape is situated 61 m. SSE of the most easterly of BAL town of the same name, in co. Cork. Area 9,711 acres. Pop. 3,971. The v. is 3\frac{1}{4} m. NW of Mallow. Pop. 576.
BACLYCLUG, a parish 2 m. SSE of Ballymena, co. Antrim. Area 8,268 acres. Pop. 4,307.
BALLYCOLLA, a village in the p. of Aghaboe, Queen's co. Pop. 168.
BALLYCOMMON, a parish 31 m. W of Philipstown, King's co. Area 6,641 acres. Pop. 1,066.

BALLYCONNELL, a small market and posttown in the p. of Tomregan, co. Cavan, 5 m. W of Belturbet. From its skirt rises the mountain of Slieve-Russel, or, as it is called in the ordnance survey, Ligavregra,-a height which attains an alt. of 1,300 ft. above sea-level, and forms a conspicuous and remarkable feature in the landscape. Pop. 387. -Also a village in the p. of Dromcliffe, co, Sligo, 95 m. NW of Sligo. Pop. 553.
BALLYCONICK, a parish 31 m. S of Taghmon, co. Wexford. Area 1,611 acres. Pop. 525.
BALLYCONRY, or BALLYCONRY-DERICO, a parish in co. Kerry. Area 1,206 acres. Pop. 417.
BALLYCOOLANE, or CLOGHRAN, or CLOGHRAN-HIDART, a small parish in co. Dublin. Area 778 acres. Pop. 111.
BALLYCOR, a parish 1 m. N by E of Ballyclare, co. Antrim. It contains the village of Ballyeaston. Area of p. 7,330 acres. Pop. 2,611.

BALLYCOTTON, a bay, some islets, and a fishing-village, in co. Cork. The bay enters between Ring-point, or Cable-island, 5 m. S by W of the entrance of Youghal-harbour, and the Ballycotton islands, which lie adjacent to a headland 5\frac{3}{4} m. SW of Ring-point. It is exposed to SE gales.—The largest of the islands is a high small spot, almost covered, at the proper season, with nests of various sea-fowls.— The v. is in the p. of Cloyne, at the mouth of a rivulet 71 m. S of Castle-Martyr. Pop. 449.
BALLYCROGUE, a parish 3 m. SE of Carlow, co. Carlow. Area 370 acres. Pop. 64.
BALLYCULTER, a parish containing the town of Strangford, in co. Down. Area 5,177 acres. Pop. 2,182.
BALLYCUMBER, a village in the p. of Lemanaghan, King's co., on the river Brosna, 23 m. SW of Clara. Pop. 231.
BALLYDEHOB, a village in the p. of Skull, co. Cork, on the road from Skibbereen to Crookhaven. Pop. 636.
BALLYDELOHER, or KILROAN, a parish 6 m. ENE of Cork, co. Cork. It contains the village of Brooklodge. Area 2,101 acres. Pop. 1,183.
BALLYDELOUGHY, or BALLYLOUGH, a parish 3½ m. SW of Mitchelstown, co. Cork. Area 2,000 acres. Pop. 796.

BALLYDONAGAN, a bay, and a fishing-v., in co. Cork. The bay indents the western extremity of the large peninsula between the Kenmare river and Bantry bay; and opens between Cod's head on the N, and Dursey island on the S. Pop. 340.
BALLYDRYHEAD, a village in the p. of Ballysadere, co. Sligo. Pop. 126.

BALLYDUFF, a parish 7 m. NE by N of Dingle, co. Kerry. Area 6,984 acres. Pop. 488-Also a village in the p. of Rattoo, co. Kerry. Pop. 331.— Also a village in the p. of Lismore and Mocollop, co. Waterford. Pop. 302.

BALLYEASTON, a quoad sacra p., containing a village of the same name, in co. Antrim. Area 13,799 acres. Pop. in 1831, 5,892. The village is situated within the quoad civilia p. of Ballycor, 13 m. N of Ballyclare. Pop. 265.

BALLYELLA, a bay and a cape, the latter wholly, and the former chiefly, in co. Clare. The APPRICATE PROPERTY CONTINUES CONTINUES CONTINUES CONTINUES CONTINUES CONTINUES CONTINUES CONTINUES CONTINUES CONTINUES CONTINUES CONTINUES CONTINUES CONTINUES CONTINUES CONTINUES CONTINUES CONTINUES CONTINUES CONTINUES CON the Arran islands; and forms the termination of a peninsula, by which the N side of the bay is screened. The bay is 35 m. wide at the entrance, and penetrates the land to the extent of 4 m. Ballyella bay is sometimes called Liscanor bay; and Ballyella point, sometimes Hagg's head. On the shore of the bay is the v. of Liscanor.
BALLYELLIN, a parish 5 m. SE by S of Goresbridge, co. Carlow. Area 4,957 acres. Pop. 1,750. The village of B. stands on the Barrow. Pop. 119.
BALLYFARNON, or BALLYFERNON, a village in the p. of Kilronan, co. Roscommon, 41 m. NW of Keadue. Pop. 243.
BALLYFEARD, a parish 41 m. NE of Kinsale, co. Cork. Area 3,462 acres. Pop. 1,113.

BALLYFERIS, a headland in the parish of St. Andrew's, co. Down, 4 m. SSE of Donaghadee, in N lat. 55° 33', and W long. 5° 23'. At the end of it a reef of rocks, called the Long rock, stretches a good way into the sea; and S of it lies another called Scalmartin, which is overflowed by the tide.
BALLYFERMOT, a parish 3 m. W by S of Dublin, co. Dublin. Area 1,183 acres. Pop. 346.
BALLYFOILE, or Poliplicke, a parish 3 m. S by W of the entrance of Cork harbour, co. Cork, Munster. Area 2,882 acres. Pop. 1,311.
BALLYGAR, a village in the p. of Killereran, co. Galway, 3 m. NE of Ballinamore. Pop. 363.
BALLYGARTH, a parish on the coast of co. Meath, 4 m. NW by N of Balbriggan. Area 799 acres. Pop. 165.

BALLYGAWLEY, a small market and post-town, in the parish of Errigal-Keerogue, co. Tyrone, on the road from Dublin to Londonderry, 3\frac{1}{4} m. NNW of Aughnacloy. The linen manufacture here engages attention; and a manufacture of gloves is in repute. Starbog spa is about 5 m. distant. Pop. 881.
BALLYGELLY, a basaltic cliff and headland in the parish of Carncastle, 44 m. N by W of Larne, in co. Antrim. It consists of basaltic strata, incipiently columnar.
BALLYGIBBON, a parish 4 m. NE of Nenagh, co. Tipperary. Area 3,294 acres. Pop. 1,321.
BALLYGOREY, a village in the p. of Portnascully, co. Kilkenny. Pop. 196.
BALLYGORMAN, a village in the p. of Cloncha, co. Donegal. Pop. about 250.
BALLYGRIFFIN, a parish 31 m. W by N of Cashel, co. Tipperary. Area 2,862 acres. Pop. 1,406.
BALLYGUB (New), a village in the p. of Clonamery, co. Kilkenny. Pop. 222.
BALLYGUNNER, a parish 12 m. SW of Passage, co. Waterford. Area 2,266 acres. Pop. 807.
BALLYGURRUM, or BALLYGORUM, a parish in co. Kilkenny, 44 m. W by S of New Ross. Area 1,924 acres. Pop. 796.
BALLYHACK, a village in the p. of St. James and Dunbrody, co. Wexford, on the E shore of Waterford harbour, 5\frac{1}{4} m. NW by N of Fethard. Pop. 266.
BALLYHACKMORE, a village in the p. of Hollywood, co. Down. Pop. 126.
BALLYHAHILL, a village in the p. of Kilmoylan, co. Limerick. Pop. 196.
BALLYHAISE, or BALLYHAYS, a small markettown in the p. of Castleterra, co. Cavan, on the Annalee river, 3 m. NNE of Cavan. Pop. 704.
BALLYHALBERT, a parish, containing a village of the same name, in co. Down. Area 4,011 acres. Pop. 2,656. The v. is situated on the coast, 9 m. S by E of Donaghadee. Pop. 361.
BALLYHALE, a village in the parish of Derrynahinch, co. Kilkenny, 4 m. S of Thomastown. Pop. 307.
BALLYHANE, or BALLYHEAN, a parish 41 m. Queen's co., 3 m. SW of Athy. Pop. 445. BAL SSW of Castlebar, co. Mayo. Area 7,674 acres. Pop. 4,032.
BALLYHAUNIS, a village or small market-town in the parishes of Annagh and Bekan, co. Mayo, 9 m. W of Castlerea.
BALLYHEA, or BALLYHAY, a parish in co. Cork, 2½ m. SSE of Charleville. Area 4,836 acres. Pop. 1,390.

BALLYHEIGUE, a promontory and a bay on the coast of co. Kerry. The promontory projects at the S side of the entrance of the Shannon; but is more commonly known under the name of Kerry head. The bay commences at this promontory on the N, and extends to Tralee bay on the S. As the beach is flat, and W winds send up tremendous billows and breakers from the whole sweep of the Atlantic, no vessel driven by stress of weather into the bay can live; yet the N part of the bay is the deepest, and should be preferred by mariners who find themselves unable to steer north of Kerry head.-Also a parish on the coast of co. Kerry, extending along the N part of the bay just described, and about 43 m. NW of Ardfert. It contains the villages of Ballyheigue, Drumgower, Coumdeehy, and Tiershanaghan. Area 11,261 acres. Pop. 4,795.
BALLYHEOGUE, or BALLYHOGUE, a parish 51 m. S by W of Enniscorthy, co. Wexford. Area 4,270 acres. Pop. 997.
BALLYHOOLEY, or AGHULTIE, a parish, containing a v. of the same name, in co. Cork. Area 5,253 acres. Pop. 2,337. The village stands on the 1. bank of the Blackwater, 4 m. W by N of Fermoy. Pop. 418.
BALLYHUSKARD, a parish 2½ m. SE by E of Enniscorthy, co. Wexford. It contains the villages of Ballaghkeen and Ballinamuddagh. Area 7,948 acres. Pop. 2,874.
BALLYINGLY, or BALLINGLY, a parish 6 m. SW by S of Taghmon, co. Wexford. Area 765 acres. Pop. 221.
BALLYJAMESDUFF, a quoad sacra p. containing a town of the same name, in co. Cavan. Area 4,739 acres. Pop. in 1831, 3,227.—The town of B. is 5 m. NW of Virginia. Pop. 1,071.
BALLYKEAN, or KILLEIGHY, a parish in King's co. Leinster, 3 m. N by W of Portarlington. Area 12,912 acres. Pop. 2,445.
BALLYKELLY, a village in the p. of Tamlaght- Finlagan, co. Londonderry, 24 m. SW of Newtown- Limavaddy. Pop. 321.
BALLYKINE, a parish in co. Wicklow. Area 11,054 acres. Pop. 2,755. The highest ground has an alt. of 1,318 ft. The hamlets are Aughrim, Ballinaclash, and Macreddin.
BALLYKINLER, a parish 2 m. SE by S of Clough, co. Down. Area 2,039 acres. Pop. 793.
BALLYKNOCKAN, a village in the p. of Fennagh, co. Carlow. Pop. 170.
BALLYLANEEN, a parish 11 m. W of Waterford, co. Waterford. It contains the village of Bonmahon, and part of the town of Kilmacthomas. Area 6,315 acres. Pop. 5,153.
BALLYLARKIN, a parish 13 m. SW of Freshford, co. Kilkenny. Area 1,394 acres. Pop. 264.
BALLYLEAGUE, a village in the p. of Clontuskert, co. Roscommon. It stands on the Shannon; and is connected by a bridge of 9 arches with Lanesborough. Pop. 278.
BALLYLENNAN, a parish 7½ m. SW of Taghmon, co. Wexford. Area 2,493 acres. Pop. 710.
BALLYLIFFIN, a village in the p. of Clonmany, co. Donegal. It stands near the Atlantic, close to the island of Doagh, 4 m. W of Carn. Pop. 193.
BALLYLINAN, a village in the p. of Killeban, セン ビデス・デース・ディー・ディー・ディー・ディー・ディー・ディー・ディー・ディー・ディー・ディー
BALLYLINCH, a parish in co. Kilkenny. Area 1,167 acres. Pop. 319.
BALLYLINNEY, a parish 11 m. S by W of Ballyclare, co. Antrim. Area 5,364 acres. Pop. 2,204.

BALLYLONGFORD, a town in the p. of Aghavallah, co. Kerry, 4 m. WSW of Tarbert. The town possesses some importance as a place of direct shipment for the produce of the neighbouring district. Pop. 1,143.
BALLYLOUGHAUN, a village in the p. of St. Nicholas, co. Galway. Pop. 180.
BALLYLOUGHLOE, a parish 4 m. NW of Moate-Grenogue. Area 13,577 acres. Pop. 4,793.
BALLYLOUGHNANE, a village in the p. of Lockeen, co. Tipperary. Pop. 344.

BALLYMACARETT, a quoad sacra p., a townland, and a town, within the quoad civilia p. of Knockbreda, co. Down, on the r. bank of the river Lagan, directly opposite Belfast, and included within its parliamentary borough limits. Its superficial extent is 576 acres. Pop. in 1831, 5,168; in 1841, 6,697.
BALLYMACART, a parish 6 m. S by E of Dungarvan, co. Waterford. Area 2,537 acres. Pop. 1,196.
BALLYMACARTHY, or BALLINASCARTHY, a village in the p. of Kilmaleda, co. Cork, 2½ m. NNE of Clonakilty. In its vicinity are very extensive corn-mills. Pop. 154.
BALLYMACDUN, or BALLYMADUN, a parish in co. Dublin, 3 m. NNE of Ratoath. Area 3,438 acres. Pop. 522. The v. of B. is 13 m. N by W of Dublin.
BALLYMACELLIGOTT, a parish 43 m. ESE of Tralee, co. Kerry. Area 14,018 acres. Pop. 4,058.
BALLYMACHUGH, a parish in co. Cavan. Area 7,728 acres. Pop. 3,518. It lies 51 m. NE of Granard.
BALLYMACKEAN, a village in the p. of Ringrone, co. Cork. Pop. 202.
BALLYMACKEY, a parish 4 m. E of Nenagh, co. Tipperary. Area 9,713 acres. Pop. in 1841, 3,178.
BALLYMACODA, a village in co. Cork, in the p. of Kilmacdonogh. Pop. 305.
BALLYMACORMACK, a parish 1½ m. S of Longford, co. Longford. Area 8,925 acres. Pop. 3,926.
BALLYMACWARD, a parish in co. Galway. Area 17,258 acres. Pop. 5,027.
